summary: Reverted a prior `.clang-format` representation change by restoring the repository-level clang-format configuration to a single inline mapping style and deleting the VS Code workspace override that duplicated the same style string. This was a tooling/config cleanup PR, not a code decompilation change.
what_changed: Changed `.clang-format` from a multi-line YAML-style list of keys back to a single braced mapping form.

Kept the same clang-format settings semantically visible in the diff: LLVM base style, left pointer alignment, 4-space indentation, no tabs, custom function brace wrapping, disabled column limit, namespace indentation, C-style cast spacing, and disabled Cpp11BracedListStyle.
Deleted `.vscode/settings.json`, removing the `C_Cpp.clang_format_style` workspace override that embedded the same clang-format style as a string.
No source, header, symbol, or matching logic files were changed.
smart_moves: Removed editor-specific duplication in `.vscode/settings.json` so formatting policy lives in the repository `.clang-format` file instead of being repeated in a VS Code-only setting.
Kept the formatting options themselves effectively unchanged while reverting the file representation, reducing risk of accidental formatting churn across decompilation files.
Limited the revert to configuration files, making the PR easy to audit and avoiding unrelated source formatting changes.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation repositories, formatting configuration changes should be treated as high-impact because they can create broad, noisy diffs that obscure matching work.
Prefer a single canonical formatting source such as `.clang-format`; duplicating style rules in editor-specific settings can drift and cause inconsistent local formatting.
Small tooling reverts can matter for preserving stable code layout, which is useful when reviewing matching-sensitive C source changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available beyond the PR title indicating this was a revert of a `.clang-format` change.
follow_up_queries: Find the prior PR or commit that changed `.clang-format` before PR 490 to understand why this revert was needed.
Search later PRs for `.clang-format` or `C_Cpp.clang_format_style` to see whether the project standardized further on formatting configuration.
Check whether deleting `.vscode/settings.json` affected contributor setup documentation or editor recommendations.
